description_long: This is a short wooden picket fence composed of vertical slats evenly spaced and connected by two horizontal rails, one at the top and one at the bottom. The wood has a light, natural finish, giving it a warm, yellowish-brown appearance. The top edge of the fence is flat, with each vertical slat cut to the same height, creating a uniform silhouette. The posts at each end are slightly thicker than the slats but have no decorative caps or finials. The construction appears sturdy but lightweight, suitable for use as a boundary in gardens, parks, or residential yards. There are no visible decorative elements or gates integrated into the design.
description: A short, light-colored wooden picket fence with evenly spaced vertical slats and flat top edges, connected by two horizontal rails. The design is simple, functional, and lacks decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the evenly spaced vertical slats and flat top edge, with both horizontal rails visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the alignment of the slats and rails.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the same slat and rail arrangement as the front, with the posts and rails clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and the structure of the rails and slats.
